info.title: ShipsGo API
info.description:
### Overview

The ShipsGo API is built on REST principles. Authenticated users can interact with any of our
URIs by using the specified HTTP request method. Our API has predictable resource-oriented URLs,
accepts JSON request bodies, returns JSON responses, and uses standard HTTP response codes,
authentication, and verbs.

### Authentication

All requests to ShipsGo API require you to authenticate yourself to the service. In order to do
this you must send `X-Shipsgo-User-Token` header with the correct API token. If you don't have an
API token, you can create one from "[ShipsGo API](https://shipsgo.com/dashboard/air/integrations/api-tokens)"
section on your dashboard.

If you use ShipsGo with multiple users (sub-accounts), API tokens are associated with a single
user. Each account has different permission on API endpoints based on their role, similar to dashboard.

| Role           | Description                                                                                 |
| -------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Full Access    | View & manage (delete/update) all shipments of the company.                                 |
| Co-Worker      | View & manage (delete/update) shipments only created by itself.                             |
| Co-Worker Plus | View all shipments of the company, manage (delete/update) shipments only created by itself. |

If you want to check permissions of your users you can view/manage from
"[Sub Accounts](https://shipsgo.com/dashboard/sub-accounts)" section on your dashboard.

### HTTP Response Codes

ShipsGo uses conventional HTTP response codes to indicate the success or failure of an API request.
In general: Codes in the `2xx` range indicate success. Codes in the `4xx` range indicate an error that
failed given the information provided. Codes in the `5xx` range indicate an error with ShipsGo's servers
(these are rare).

| Code  | Description           | Reason                                                                                          |
| ----- | --------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `200` | Success               | Everything went smooth.                                                                         |
| `401` | Unauthorized          | Missing or invalid authentication token.                                                        |
| `402` | Payment Required      | Not enough credits.                                                                             |
| `403` | Forbidden             | User not authorized to perform the operation.                                                   |
| `404` | Not Found             | Requested resource is not found.                                                                |
| `409` | Conflict              | The requested resource already exists.                                                          |
| `422` | Unprocessable Content | Something with the payload isn't quite right, this could be malformed JSON or incorrect fields. |
| `429` | Too Many Requests     | Client has sent too many requests in a given amount of time.                                    |
| `500` | Internal Server Error | The server encountered an unexpected condition.                                                 |
| `503` | Service Unavailable   | The server is down for maintenance. This is a temporary state.                                  |

### Rate Limits

The ShipsGo API uses a number of safeguards against bursts of incoming traffic to help maximize its stability.
For your company, the API rate limit is **100 requests per minute**. This limit applies collectively across all
users within the company. You can use the headers that are sent with each response to determine the current status
of your rate limit. If you send many requests in a short window, you might see error responses with status code `429`.

| Header                | Description                                                                   |
| --------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| X-RateLimit-Limit     | The maximum number of requests that you can make per minute.                  |
| X-RateLimit-Remaining | The number of requests remaining in the current rate limit window.            |
| X-RateLimit-Reset     | The time at which the current rate limit window resets, in UTC epoch seconds. |

### List Endpoints

All top-level API resources have support for bulk fetches. These list endpoints share a common structure for
filtering, sorting and pagination. For example, you can list your air shipments with upcoming arrivals in order:

```plain
[GET] /air/shipments?filters[status]=eq:EN_ROUTE&order_by=date_of_rcf,asc
```

There are a variety of different filters that can be used to each list enpoint. Filters can be used together in
different combinations. When multiple filters are applied, they will be combined using the **AND** condition. Common
structure is `filters[field]=operator:value(s)`. Each `field` has corresponding `operator`s. You can find the
`field` & `operator` mappings in the **filters** section of the relevant endpoint. The following operators are
supported:

| Operator       | Description                                                               |
| -------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `eq`           | Equal to a specific value.                                                |
| `not_eq`       | Not equal to a specific value.                                            |
| `contains`     | Checks if the field value contains a specific substring.                  |
| `not_contains` | Checks if the field value does not contain a specific substring.          |
| `in`           | Checks if the field value is within a list of values (`,` separated).     |
| `not_in`       | Checks if the field value is not within a list of values (`,` separated). |
| `with`         | Checks if the field value is within a list of values (`,` separated).     |
| `without`      | Checks if the field value is not within a list of values (`,` separated). |
| `gt`           | Greater than a specific value.                                            |
| `gte`          | Greater than or equal to a specific value.                                |
| `lt`           | Less than a specific value.                                               |
| `lte`          | Less than or equal to a specific value.                                   |
| `between`      | Checks if the field value is between two values (`...` separated).        |

You can sort the list with `order_by` parameter. Common structure is `order_by=field,direction`. You can find the
available `field` list in the **order_by** section of the relevant endpoint and `direction` can be `asc` (ascending)
or `desc` (descending). If you omit the `direction`, the default sort direction is `asc` (ascending).

For the pagination you can use `skip` & `take` parameters with non-negative integer values.

### Webhooks

When an event occurs, our system automatically sends a HTTP request (POST) to your specified webhook endpoint with
relevant data. This allows you to receive **real-time updates** without manually checking for changes. For example,
when a shipment is created or updated, your system can instantly process the information and take the necessary
actions. You can manage your webhooks & events you want to listen from
"[Webhooks](https://shipsgo.com/dashboard/air/integrations/webhooks)" section on your dashboard.

This is the recommended way to track shipments via the API. You should use webhooks instead of polling our API
periodically.

##### Retry Mechanism

If your webhook endpoint responds with a server error (`5xx`) or connection error occurs, we automatically retry
delivery up to 3 additional times. This helps to prevent losting webhook events in case of temporary failures. Retries
are based on the following schedule;

1. **1 minute** after first failure.
2. **5 minutes** after second failure.
3. **10 minutes** after third failure (last attempt).

##### Validating Webhook Payloads (Optional)

ShipsGo will use your **Secret Key** to create a hash signature that's sent to you with each payload. The hash
signature is generated using **HMAC-SHA256** and will appear in each delivery as the value of the
`X-Shipsgo-Webhook-Signature` header.

In your code that handles webhook deliveries, you should calculate a hash using your **Secret Key**. Then, compare
the hash that ShipsGo sent with the expected hash that you calculated, and ensure that they match.

There are a few important things to keep in mind when validating webhook payloads:

- ShipsGo uses an HMAC hex digest to compute the hash.
- The hash signature is generated using your Secret Key and the payload contents.
- If your language and server implementation specifies a character encoding, ensure that you handle the payload as
  UTF-8. Webhook payloads can contain unicode characters.
- Never use a plain `==` operator. Instead consider using a method performs a "constant time" string comparison to
  help mitigate certain timing attacks.

You can use the following secret and payload values to verify that your implementation is correct:

**Secret Key:** `SUPER_LONG_AND_SECURE_SECRET_KEY`

**Webhook Payload:** `{"message":"You shall not pass!"}`

If your implementation is correct, the signatures that you generate should match the following signature:

**Signature:** `9527e0c9463e6f5b01a0af50aecb4658ff50c6b25d3efa8e5c8dea7e4b763772`

---

- [Pricing](https://shipsgo.com/pricing)
- [Terms & Conditions](https://shipsgo.com/terms)
- [Privacy Policy](https://shipsgo.com/privacy-policy)
info.x-cli-description: Every ShipsGo endpoint, plus an RFQ workspace, lane analytics, and an offline shipment book.

tags.name: air-airlines
tags.description: This section provides a comprehensive set of endpoints for airlines.

tags.name: air-shipments
tags.description:
This section provides a comprehensive set of endpoints to manage your air shipments.

### How It Works

First, you need to create the shipment, and after that, we will periodically check the shipment status and other
relevant details. Our system will automatically track the shipment's progress and any changes, ensuring everything stays
updated. You will be notified with updates and changes as needed, ensuring timely and accurate information about your
shipment. You can also check your shipment details at any time, or use webhooks to receive real-time updates.

tags.name: air-webhook-events
tags.description:
This section provides a comprehensive set of webhook events to manage your air shipments. You can check the
**[Webhooks](#description/webhooks)** section for more information about intergration and security.

tags.name: ocean-carriers
tags.description: This section provides a comprehensive set of endpoints for carriers.

tags.name: ocean-shipments
tags.description:

Token.name: X-Shipsgo-User-Token
Token.description: The API token associated with your user.

id.description: The unique identifier for the tag.
The unique identifier for the follower.

email.description: The email adress that will be notified about the shipment.

iata.description: 2 unique digits that is assigned to the airline.

status.description:
Specifies the trackability status of the airline.

* `ACTIVE` - The airline is trackable.
* `PASSIVE` - The airline is not trackable.

**Note**: Airline status may change.

prefixes.description: The air waybill (AWB) prefix(es) for the airline.

items.description: The air waybill (AWB) prefix for the airline.

iata.description: Unique 3-letter code that identifies the airport.

code.description: Unique 2-letter code that identifies the country.

id.description: The unique identifier of the shipment.

reference.description: Your reference to remember or classify the shipment.

awb_number.description: The Air Waybill (AWB) number provided by the airline.

iata.description: 2 unique digits that is assigned to the airline.

airline.description: The airline that is responsible for the shipment.

pieces.description: The number of pieces in the shipment.

weight.description: The weight of the shipment in kilograms (kg).

volume.description: The volume of the shipment in cubic meters (m<sup>3</sup>).

cargo.description: The quantitative properties of the shipment.

status.description:
The status to represent current state of the shipment.
* `NEW` - The shipment has not been checked by the ShipsGo system yet.
* `INPROGRESS` - The airline hasn't provided any information about the shipment yet. ShipsGo system will continue to check periodically.
* `BOOKED` - The shipment has been reserved with the airline for transport, but it has not yet been loaded or dispatched.
* `EN_ROUTE` - The shipment is in transit and on its way to the final destination.
* `LANDED` - The shipment's flight has arrived at the destination airport.
* `DELIVERED` - The shipmen has been completed and delivered to consignee.
* `UNTRACKED` - There is no available tracking information for the shipment. The AWB number might be invalid, or the airline may not have a public tracking system.

status_split.description: It states whether the shipment is split/partial cargo or not.

status_extended.description:
Shipment status based on number of pieces. Each key of the object is one of the `status` values.

In the case of a split shipment, this field will be helpful for you to monitor the different statuses
of the pieces.

**Example #1**: Out of 5 pieces, 5 pieces are **EN ROUTE**.
```json
{
    "EN_ROUTE": 5
}
```

**Example #2**: Out of 5 pieces, 3 pieces are **EN ROUTE**, 2 pieces have **LANDED**.
```json
{
    "EN_ROUTE": 3,
    "LANDED": 2
}
```

date_of_dep.description: Departure date and time of the shipment from the origin port.

date_of_dep_initial.description: The first departure date and time of the shipment from the origin port.

origin.description: The origin airport where the shipment will be loaded or has been loaded from.

ts_count.description: The number of transshipment points in the route, representing intermediate stops where the shipment is transferred from one flight to another.

date_of_rcf.description: Recieved from flight date and time at destination location.

date_of_rcf_initial.description: The first recieved from flight date and time at destination location.

destination.description: The destination airport where the shipment will arrive or has arrived to.

transit_time.description: Total transit time of the shipment, from origin to destination.

transit_percentage.description: Percentage of the completed part of the journey over total transit time.

route.description: The route details of the shipment, including origin, destination, and transit information.

movements.description: A list of movements, listing all the events that will occur or has occurred.

map.description:
Token for the link where people can see the shipment details and current location on the
[ShipsGo Map](https://map.shipsgo.com). You can create a link like the one below.

```text
https://map.shipsgo.com/air/shipments/{shipment_id}?token={map_token}
```

tokens.description: A list of tokens you can use to create publicly accessible links to the shipment.

followers.description: Email addresses subscribed to receive notifications about the shipment.

tags.description: A list of labels or keywords associated with the shipment.

updated_at.description: Date of the last update (UTC). It only covers movement and status changes.

checked_at.description: Date of the last time shipment checked by ShipsGo (UTC).

discarded_at.description:
Indicates the timestamp (UTC) when the shipment was marked as discarded from the system's active control
queue. Once a shipment is discarded, the system will no longer track or update the shipment. If it's `null`
(empty), it means the shipment is still actively being tracked, and the system will continue to update it.

id.description: The unique identifier of the shipment.

reference.description: Your reference to remember or classify the shipment.

awb_number.description: The Air Waybill (AWB) number provided by the airline.

iata.description: 2 unique digits that is assigned to the airline.

airline.description: The airline that is responsible for the shipment.

pieces.description: The number of pieces in the shipment.

weight.description: The weight of the shipment in kilograms (kg).

volume.description: The volume of the shipment in cubic meters (m<sup>3</sup>).

cargo.description: The quantitative properties of the shipment.

status.description:
The status to represent current state of the shipment.
* `NEW` - The shipment has not been checked by the ShipsGo system yet.
* `INPROGRESS` - The airline hasn't provided any information about the shipment yet. ShipsGo system will continue to check periodically.
* `BOOKED` - The shipment has been reserved with the airline for transport, but it has not yet been loaded or dispatched.
* `EN_ROUTE` - The shipment is in transit and on its way to the final destination.
* `LANDED` - The shipment's flight has arrived at the destination airport.
* `DELIVERED` - The shipmen has been completed and delivered to consignee.
* `UNTRACKED` - There is no available tracking information for the shipment. The AWB number might be invalid, or the airline may not have a public tracking system.

status_split.description: It states whether the shipment is split/partial cargo or not.

date_of_dep.description: Departure date and time of the shipment from the origin port.

date_of_dep_initial.description: The first departure date and time of the shipment from the origin port.

origin.description: The origin airport where the shipment will be loaded or has been loaded from.

ts_count.description: The number of transshipment points in the route, representing intermediate stops where the shipment is transferred from one flight to another.

date_of_rcf.description: Recieved from flight date and time at destination location.

date_of_rcf_initial.description: The first recieved from flight date and time at destination location.

destination.description: The destination airport where the shipment will arrive or has arrived to.

transit_time.description: Total transit time of the shipment, from origin to destination.

transit_percentage.description: Percentage of the completed part of the journey over total transit time.

route.description: The route details of the shipment, including origin, destination, and transit information.

tags.description: A list of labels or keywords associated with the shipment.

updated_at.description: Date of the last update (UTC). It only covers movement and status changes.

checked_at.description: Date of the last time shipment checked by ShipsGo (UTC).

discarded_at.description:
Indicates the timestamp (UTC) when the shipment was marked as discarded from the system's active control
queue. Once a shipment is discarded, the system will no longer track or update the shipment. If it's `null`
(empty), it means the shipment is still actively being tracked, and the system will continue to update it.

id.description: The unique identifier of the shipment.

reference.description: Your reference to remember or classify the shipment.

awb_number.description: The Air Waybill (AWB) number provided by the airline and required to track the shipment.

event.description:
The event codes that represent type of the movement.
* `RCS` - Received from Agent Or Shipper.
* `MAN` - Manifested.
* `DEP` - Departed.
* `ARR` - Arrived.
* `RCF` - Received from Flight.
* `DLV` - Delivered.

status.description:
Status of the movement. It will state whether the given timestamp of the movement is estimation or actual.
* `EST` - Estimated which means that the given timestamp of the movement is an estimation. The movement is planned to be occur at the given timestamp.
* `ACT` - Actual which means that the given timestamp of the movement is actual. The movement has already occurred at the given timestamp.

pieces.description: The number of pieces that are present on the event.

weight.description: The weight of the pieces that is present on the event in kilograms (kg).

volume.description: The volume of the pieces that is present on the event in cubic meters (m<sup>3</sup>).

cargo.description: The quantitative properties of the shipment that goes through the event.

flight.description:
Flight number.

**Note:** It's always `null` for `RCS` and `DLV` events.

timestamp.description: The time of the movement (ISO 8601 with timezone). It is an estimated or actual time, depending on the status of the movement.

AirGeoJSONPoint.description: A `Point` feature representing a specific location along the shipment route.

type.description: Type of the feature, which is `Feature` always.
Type of the geometry, which is `Point` for this feature.

coordinates.description: An array representing the coordinates (`[lng, lat]`) of the `Point`.

AirGeoJSONLineString.description: A `LineString` feature representing a segment of the shipment route.

type.description: Type of the feature, which is `Feature` always.
Type of the geometry, which is `LineString` for this feature.

coordinates.description: An array of coordinate pairs (`[[lng, lat], ..., [lng, lat]]`) representing the `LineString`.

items.description: An array representing the coordinates (`[lng, lat]`) of the `Point`.

cargo.description: The quantitative properties of the shipment for this route segment.

pieces.description: The number of pieces in the shipment.

weight.description: The weight of the shipment in kilograms (kg).

volume.description: The volume of the shipment in cubic meters (m³).

flight.description: The flight number associated with this route segment.

events.description: Key events associated with this route segment.

timestamp.description: The timestamp of the departure event in ISO 8601 format.
The timestamp of the arrival event in ISO 8601 format.

current.description: The current position of the shipment along this route segment if applicable (`status` is `CURRENT`).

index.description: The index of the current position in the coordinates array. You can split the route (coordinates array) at this index to get the `PAST` and `FUTURE` segments.

coordinates.description: The current coordinates (`[lng, lat]`) of the shipment.

scac.description: The SCAC code of the carrier.

status.description:
Specifies the trackability status of the carrier.

* `ACTIVE` - The carrier is trackable.
* `PASSIVE` - The carrier is not trackable.

**Note**: Carrier status may change.

code.description: Unique 5-letter code (first 2 letters belong to country code) that identifies the ocean port.
Unique 2-letter code that identifies the country.

id.description: The unique identifier of the shipment.

reference.description: Your reference to remember or classify the shipment.

booking_number.description: The booking number of the shipment.

container_number.description: The container number of the shipment.

container_count.description: The number of containers associated with the shipment.

scac.description: The Standard Carrier Alpha Code (SCAC) of the carrier.

carrier.description: The carrier that is responsible for the shipment.

status.description:
The status to represent current state of the shipment.
* `NEW` - The shipment has not been checked by the ShipsGo system yet.
* `INPROGRESS` - The carrier hasn't provided any information about the shipment yet. ShipsGo system will continue to check periodically.
* `BOOKED` - The shipment has been reserved with the carrier for transport, but it has not yet been loaded.
* `LOADED` - The shipment has been loaded onto the vessel, but it has not yet departed from the port of loading.
* `SAILING` - The shipment is currently in transit, on its way to the port of discharge.
* `ARRIVED` - The shipment's vessel has arrived at the port of discharge, but the shipment has not yet been discharged from the vessel.
* `DISCHARGED` - The shipment has been discharged from the vessel at the port of discharge.
* `UNTRACKED` - There is no available tracking information for the shipment. Container/MBL/Booking number might be invalid, or the carrier may not have a public tracking system.

date_of_loading.description: The date when the shipment was loaded onto the vessel.

date_of_loading_initial.description: The first declared loading date provided by the carrier.

ts_count.description: The number of transshipments for the shipment.

date_of_discharge.description: The date when the shipment is expected to be discharged.

date_of_discharge_initial.description: The first declared discharge date provided by the carrier

transit_time.description: The estimated transit time for the shipment in days.

transit_percentage.description: The percentage of the shipment's transit time that has been completed.

co2_emission.description: It provides estimate total emissions values for the shipment(in tons)

containers.description: The details of the containers associated with the shipment, including each container's statuses, movement info, number, type, size, and other relevant information.

map.description:
Token for the link where people can see the shipment details and current location on the
[ShipsGo Map](https://map.shipsgo.com). You can create a link like the one below.

```text
https://map.shipsgo.com/ocean/shipments/{shipment_id}?token={map_token}
```

followers.description: Email addresses subscribed to receive notifications about the shipment.

tags.description: A list of labels or keywords associated with the shipment.

Indicates the timestamp (UTC) when the shipment was marked as discarded from the system's active control
queue. Once a shipment is discarded, the system will no longer track or update the shipment. If it's `null`
(empty), it means the shipment is still actively being tracked, and the system will continue to update it.

reference.description: Your reference to remember or classify the shipment.

booking_number.description: The booking number of the shipment.

container_number.description: The container number of the shipment.

container_count.description: The number of containers associated with the shipment.

scac.description: The Standard Carrier Alpha Code (SCAC) of the carrier.

carrier.description: The carrier that is responsible for the shipment.

status.description:
The status to represent current state of the shipment.
* `NEW` - The shipment has not been checked by the ShipsGo system yet.
* `INPROGRESS` - The carrier hasn't provided any information about the shipment yet. ShipsGo system will continue to check periodically.
* `BOOKED` - The shipment has been reserved with the carrier for transport, but it has not yet been loaded.
* `LOADED` - The shipment has been loaded onto the vessel, but it has not yet departed from the port of loading.
* `SAILING` - The shipment is currently in transit, on its way to the port of discharge.
* `ARRIVED` - The shipment's vessel has arrived at the port of discharge, but the shipment has not yet been discharged from the vessel.
* `DISCHARGED` - The shipment has been discharged from the vessel at the port of discharge.
* `UNTRACKED` - There is no available tracking information for the shipment. Container/MBL/Booking number might be invalid, or the carrier may not have a public tracking system.

date_of_loading.description: The date when the shipment was loaded onto the vessel.

date_of_loading_initial.description: The first declared loading date provided by the carrier.

ts_count.description: The number of transshipments for the shipment.

date_of_discharge.description: The date when the shipment is expected to be discharged.

date_of_discharge_initial.description: The first declared discharge date provided by the carrier

transit_time.description: The estimated transit time for the shipment in days.

transit_percentage.description: The percentage of the shipment's transit time that has been completed.

co2_emission.description: It provides estimate total emissions values for the shipment(in tons)

map.description:
Token for the link where people can see the shipment details and current location on the
[ShipsGo Map](https://map.shipsgo.com). You can create a link like the one below.

```text
https://map.shipsgo.com/ocean/shipments/{shipment_id}?token={map_token}
```

tokens.description: A list of tokens you can use to create publicly accessible links to the shipment.

tags.description: A list of labels or keywords associated with the shipment.

updated_at.description: Date of the last update (UTC). It only covers movement and status changes.

checked_at.description: Date of the last time shipment checked by ShipsGo (UTC).

discarded_at.description:
Indicates the timestamp (UTC) when the shipment was marked as discarded from the system's active control
queue. Once a shipment is discarded, the system will no longer track or update the shipment. If it's `null`
(empty), it means the shipment is still actively being tracked, and the system will continue to update it.

id.description: The unique identifier of the shipment.

reference.description: Your reference to remember or classify the shipment.

container_number.description: The container number provided by the shipping line and required to track the shipment.

booking_number.description: The booking number provided by the shipping line.

number.description: The container number.(It can be `NOT_ASSIGNED` if the container is not assigned yet.)

status.description:
Shows the current status of the container based on its latest movements. Each container can have itsown status depending on its position in the shipping process, allowing for tracking of each container separately.
* `EMPTY_SHIPPER` - The empty container has been delivered to the shipper for the purpose of stuffing.
* `GATE_IN` - The container has entered the port or terminal and is awaiting further processing.
* `LOADED` - The container has been loaded onto the vessel but the vessel has not yet departed.
* `SAILING` - The container is in transit, on its way to the port of discharge.
* `ARRIVED` - The container's vessel has arrived at the port of discharge, but the container has not yet been discharged.
* `DISCHARGED` - The container has been unloaded from the vessel at the port of discharge and is awaiting further processing.
* `GATE_OUT` - The container has been released from the port of discharge.
* `EMPTY_RETURN` - The empty container has been returned to the designated depot or terminal after being unloaded.
* `UNKNOWN` - The status of the container is unknown. It has not yet been updated or tracked.

movements.description: List of movements of the container.

event.description:
The event codes that represent type of the movement.
* `EMSH` - Empty to Shipper.
* `GTIN` - Gate In.
* `LOAD` - Loaded.
* `DEPA` - Departed.
* `ARRV` - Arrived.
* `DISC` - Discharged.
* `GTOT` - Gate Out.
* `EMRT` - Empty Return.

status.description:
Status of the movement. It will state whether the given timestamp of the movement is estimation or actual.
* `EST` -Estimated which means that the given timestamp of the movement is an estimation. The movement is planned to be occur at the given timestamp.
* `ACT` - Actual which means that the given timestamp of the movement is actual. The movement has already occurred at the given timestamp.

timestamp.description: The time of the movement (ISO 8601 with timezone). It is an estimated or actual time, depending on the status of the movement.

OceanGeoJSONPoint.description: A `Point` feature representing a specific location along the shipment route.

type.description: Type of the feature, which is `Feature` always.
Type of the geometry, which is `Point` for this feature.

coordinates.description: An array representing the coordinates (`[lng, lat]`) of the `Point`.

OceanGeoJSONLineString.description: A `LineString` feature representing a segment of the shipment route.

type.description: Type of the feature, which is `Feature` always.
Type of the geometry, which is `LineString` for this feature.

coordinates.description: An array of coordinate pairs (`[[lng, lat], ..., [lng, lat]]`) representing the `LineString`.

items.description: An array representing the coordinates (`[lng, lat]`) of the `Point`.

vessel.description: Details of the vessel of this route segment.

voyage.description: Voyage identifier for this route segment.

events.description: Key events associated with this route segment.

timestamp.description: The timestamp of the departure event in ISO 8601 format.
The timestamp of the arrival event in ISO 8601 format.

current.description: The current position of the shipment along this route segment if applicable (`status` is `CURRENT`).

index.description: The index of the current position in the coordinates array. You can split the route (coordinates array) at this index to get the `PAST` and `FUTURE` segments.

coordinates.description: The current coordinates (`[lng, lat]`) of the shipment.

id.description: The unique indentifier of the event.

triggered_by.description:
The user that trigger the event.

**Note:** Events that are triggered by system will have `noreply@shipsgo.com` as email.

get.description:
The shipments list endpoint allows you to retrieve a list of airlines. This includes options to apply various
filters and sorting.

**Example #1:** To filter the trackable airlines.

```plain
/air/airlines?filters[status]=eq:ACTIVE
```

**Example #2:** To filter airlines that contain `ARABIA` in their name.

```plain
/air/airlines?filters[name]=contains:ARABIA
```

schema.description:
You can use the following filters with their respective operators. Common structure is
`filters[field]=operator:value(s)`.

| Field    | Operators                        | Description                                                      |
| -------- | -------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------- |
| iata     | `eq`                             | The IATA code of the airline. Ex: `TK`, `QR`, `AF`, `LH`         |
| name     | `eq`, `contains`, `not_contains` | The name of the airline.                                         |
| status   | `eq`                             | Enum: `ACTIVE`, `PASSIVE`                                        |
| prefixes | `with`, `without`                | The air waybill(AWB) prefix(es) for airline(s). Ex: `235`, `176` |

You can sort the list with following fields. Common structure is `order_by=field,direction`.

| Field | Description                   |
| ----- | ----------------------------- |
| iata  | The IATA code of the airline. |
| name  | The name of the airline.      |

**Note:** By default the results are ordered by the `name` in `asc` (ascending) order.
The number of records to skip before returning the results. The default value is `0`.
The number of records to return. The default value is `25`.

200.description: List of airlines have been successfully retrieved.

meta.description: Meta information about the returned list.

more.description: Indicates whether there are more resources to fetch.

total.description: Indicates the total number of resources, it's only available when there is no more resources.

post.description:
This endpoint allows you to create a new shipment by providing the necessary shipment details. Once
the shipment is successfully created, you need to save the given shipment identifier in your internal system
to use it across the related endpoints.

### Duplicate Shipments

If there is an another shipment with the same `reference` and `awb_number`, the shipment will not be created
(there is no cost), and the system will return a response (`409` - `ALREADY_EXISTS`) indicating details of the
existing shipment. If you don't provide a `reference` field on your request, the system will only check with
the `awb_number`.

**Note:** If you want to add a follower or tag to the existing shipment, you must make new request(s)
to related endpoints using the existing shipment's `id`.

### Concurrent Requests

We process creation requests one by one for your company to prevent race conditions, such as misusing your credits
or creating unnecessary duplicate shipments. You don't need to take any action, this process is handled entirely by
ShipsGo system. However, sending too many concurrent requests can result in longer wait times and errors
(`429` - `TOO_MANY_CONCURRENT_REQUESTS`). If you plan to create a large number of shipments at once, ensure that
requests are sent synchronously.

reference.description:
Your reference to remember or classify the shipment. You can enter any numbers or letters.

**Note:** Also it will be the default title of tracking notification emails.

awb_number.description:
The Air Waybill (AWB) number provided by the airline and required to track the shipment.

**Note:** To avoid any confusion, our system will standardize the AWB number with a
separating hyphen (`333-88888888`).

followers.description: Email addresses subscribed to receive notifications about the shipment.

tags.description:
A list of labels or keywords associated with the shipment. Used to include additional
information to the shipment in order to differentiate, identify and categorise your shipments.

200.description: Shipment successfully created & tracking process has been started.

X-Shipsgo-Credits-Cost.description: The amount of credit consumption for the operation.

X-Shipsgo-Credits-Remaining.description: The amount of credit remaining after the operation.

402.description: Company does not have enought credits to create a shipment.

X-Shipsgo-Credits-Cost.description: The amount of credit consumption for the operation.

X-Shipsgo-Credits-Remaining.description: The amount of credit remaining after the operation.

get.description:
The shipments list endpoint allows you to retrieve a list of your shipments (**basic information**). This includes
options to apply various filters and sorting. To access detailed information (`status_extended`, `movements`,
`followers`, etc.) about a shipment, you should use the **AIR - Details of the Shipment** endpoint.

**Example #1:** **Ongoing** (`EN_ROUTE`) shipments carried by **TURKISH CARGO** (`TK`) or **LUFTHANSA CARGO** (`LH`)
with upcoming arrivals in order.

```plain
/air/shipments
  ?filters[status]=eq:EN_ROUTE
  &filters[airline]=in:TK,LH
  &order_by=date_of_rcf,asc
```

**Example #2:** Shipments that were shipped to **India** (`IN`), between **September 1, 2024**, and
**October 1, 2024** in order.

```plain
/air/shipments
  ?filters[destination_country]=eq:IN
  &filters[date_of_dep]=between:2024-09-01...2024-10-01
  &order_by=date_of_dep,asc
```

**Example #3:** Shipments tagged with `COMPANY_ABC` and created by **John Doe** (`john-doe@example.com`).

```plain
/air/shipments
  ?filters[tags]=with:COMPANY_ABC
  &filters[creator]=eq:john-doe@example.com
```

schema.description:
You can use the following filters with their respective operators. Common structure is `filters[field]=operator:value(s)`.

| Field                 | Operators                                 | Description                                                                          |
| --------------------- | ----------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `id`                  | `eq`                                      | Unique identifier for the shipment.                                                  |
| `status`              | `eq`, `not_eq`, `in`, `not_in`            | Enum: `NEW`, `INPROGRESS`, `BOOKED`, `EN_ROUTE`, `LANDED`, `DELIVERED`, `UNTRACKED`. |
| `airline`             | `eq`, `not_eq`, `in`, `not_in`            | The IATA code of the airline(s). Example: `TK`, `QR`, `AF`, `LH`.                    |
| `awb_number`          | `eq`                                      | Must be in format `333-88888888` or `33388888888`.                                   |
| `reference`           | `eq`, `contains`, `not_contains`          | Custom reference used to classify shipment(s).                                       |
| `status_split`        | `eq`                                      | Boolean (`true` or `false`).                                                         |
| `origin_port`         | `eq`, `not_eq`, `in`, `not_in`            | The IATA code of the airport(s). Example: `IST`, `MIA`, `FRA`.                       |
| `date_of_dep`         | `eq`, `gt`, `gte`, `lt`, `lte`, `between` | Departure date in ISO 8601 format.                                                   |
| `date_of_dep_initial` | `eq`, `gt`, `gte`, `lt`, `lte`, `between` | Initial Departure date in ISO 8601 format.                                           |
| `origin_country`      | `eq`, `not_eq`, `in`, `not_in`            | The unique 2-letter country code. Example: `TR`, `US`, `ES`.                         |
| `destination_port`    | `eq`, `not_eq`, `in`, `not_in`            | The IATA code of the airport(s). Example: `IST`, `MIA`, `FRA`.                       |
| `date_of_rcf`         | `eq`, `gt`, `gte`, `lt`, `lte`, `between` | RCF date in ISO 8601 format.                                                         |
| `date_of_rcf_initial` | `eq`, `gt`, `gte`, `lt`, `lte`, `between` | Initial RCF date in ISO 8601 format.                                                 |
| `destination_country` | `eq`, `not_eq`, `in`, `not_in`            | The unique 2-letter country code. Example: `TR`, `US`, `ES`.                         |
| `followers`           | `with`, `without`                         | Valid email addresses.                                                               |
| `tags`                | `with`, `without`                         | Labels or keywords associated with the shipment(s).                                  |
| `creator`             | `eq`, `not_eq`, `in`, `not_in`            | Valid email addresses.                                                               |
| `created_at`          | `eq`, `gt`, `gte`, `lt`, `lte`, `between` | Creation date in ISO 8601 format.                                                    |
| `updated_at`          | `eq`, `gt`, `gte`, `lt`, `lte`, `between` | Update date in ISO 8601 format.                                                      |
| `checked_at`          | `eq`, `gt`, `gte`, `lt`, `lte`, `between` | Last check date in ISO 8601 format.                                                  |
| `discarded_at`        | `eq`, `gt`, `gte`, `lt`, `lte`, `between` | Discard date in ISO 8601 format.                                                     |

**Note:** You can specify a timezone for date fields; if no timezone is provided, they will be considered as UTC.

You can sort the list with following fields. Common structure is `order_by=field,direction`.

| Field       | Description                                  |
| ----------- | -------------------------------------------- |
| id          | Unique identifier for the shipment.          |
| status      | Current status of the shipment.              |
| airline     | Associated airline for the shipment.         |
| date_of_dep | Departure (DEP) date of the shipment.        |
| date_of_rcf | Received from flight (RCF) date.             |
| created_at  | Date and time the shipment was created.      |
| updated_at  | Date and time the shipment was last updated. |
| checked_at  | Date and time the shipment was last checked. |

**Note:** By default the results are ordered by the `created_at` in `desc` (descending) order.
The number of records to skip before returning results. Default: 0.
The number of records to return. Default: 25.

200.description: List of shipments have been successfully retrieved.

meta.description: Meta information about the returned list.

more.description: Indicates whether there are more resources to fetch.

total.description: Indicates the total number of resources, it's only available when there is no more resources.

get.description:
This endpoint provides a GeoJSON FeatureCollection with all map-related data for a shipment, including airport
locations, the aircraft’s current position, and past/future paths.

GeoJSON ([RFC 7946](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7946)) is a format used to describe geographic data and can
be directly used with most mapping libraries (Leaflet, Mapbox, Google Maps, etc.).

In this endpoint, only `Point` and `LineString` geometry types are used. Each geometry is returned within a Feature as
defined below:

-   Each Feature has a `geometry` (`Point` or `LineString`).
-   Each Feature has a set of `properties` according to its `geometry`.
-   Each Feature has a `status` property, which can be:
    -   `PAST` – The shipment was at this airport/route in the past.
    -   `CURRENT` – The shipment is at this airport/route now.
    -   `FUTURE` – The shipment is expected to be at this airport/route in the future.

#### Point Features

Features with `Point` geometry represent airports. Each `Point` Feature includes the airport’s name, IATA code,
timezone, and country information.

#### LineString Features

Features with `LineString` geometry represent routes between two locations. Each `LineString` Feature includes cargo
information, flight number, related events (`DEP`, `ARR`), and current position (when `status` is `CURRENT`).

parameters.name: shipment_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the shipment.

200.description: Route of the shipment have been successfully retrieved.

geojson.description: GeoJSON feature collection representing the shipment route.

type.description: Type of the GeoJSON object, which is `FeatureCollection` always.

features.description: An array of GeoJSON features representing points and route segments. Each feature has a `geometry` (`Point` for locations or `LineString` for route segments) and `properties` describing it's details.

filters and sorting.

**Example #1:** To filter the trackable carriers.

```plain
/ocean/carriers?filters[status]=eq:ACTIVE
```

**Example #2:** To filter carriers that contain `CMA` in their name.

```plain
/ocean/carriers?filters[name]=contains:CMA
```

schema.description:
You can use the following filters with their respective operators. Common structure is
`filters[field]=operator:value(s)`.

| Field  | Operators                        | Description                                                 |
| ------ | -------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- |
| code   | `eq`                             | The SCAC code of the carrier. Ex: `OOLU`, `MSCU`, `SG_TFEG` |
| name   | `eq`, `contains`, `not_contains` | The name of the carrier.                                    |
| status | `eq`                             | Enum: `ACTIVE`, `PASSIVE`                                   |

You can sort the list with following fields. Common structure is `order_by=field,direction`.

| Field | Description                   |
| ----- | ----------------------------- |

| name  | The name of the carrier.      |

**Note:** By default the results are ordered by the `name` in `asc` (ascending) order.

post.description:
This endpoint allows you to create a new shipment by providing the necessary shipment details. Once
the shipment is successfully created, you need to save the given shipment identifier in your internal system
to use it across the related endpoints.

### Duplicate Shipments

If a shipment already exists with the same `reference` and `booking_number` or `container_number`, the system will not
create a new shipment (no cost will be incurred) and will return a (`409 - ALREADY_EXISTS`) response containing the
details of the existing shipment.

- If the `reference` is provided in the request, it will always be considered during the duplicate check.
- If both `booking_number` and `container_number` are provided, only the `booking_number` will be considered for the duplicate check.
- If the `booking_number` is not provided, the system will perform the duplicate check using the `container_number`.

**Note:** To add a follower or tag to an existing shipment, you must send a new request to the relevant endpoints using
the existing shipment’s `id`.

### Concurrent Requests

We process creation requests one by one for your company to prevent race conditions, such as misusing your credits
or creating unnecessary duplicate shipments. You don't need to take any action, this process is handled entirely by
ShipsGo system. However, sending too many concurrent requests can result in longer wait times and errors
(`429` - `TOO_MANY_CONCURRENT_REQUESTS`). If you plan to create a large number of shipments at once, ensure that
requests are sent synchronously.

reference.description:
Your reference to remember or classify the shipment. You can enter any numbers or letters.

**Note:** Also it will be the default title of tracking notification emails.

container_number.description:
The container number provided by the shipping line and required to track the shipment.

**Note:** Either `container_number` or `booking_number` must be provided.
The `container_number` is required if `booking_number` is not provided.

booking_number.description:
The booking number provided by the shipping line and required to track the shipment.

**Note:** Either `container_number` or `booking_number` must be provided.
The `booking_number` is required if `container_number` is not provided.

carrier.description:
The SCAC code of the carrier. This is used to identify the carrier for tracking purposes.

A list of supported carriers can be obtained through the following API: **_[GET /ocean/carriers]_**

followers.description: Email addresses subscribed to receive notifications about the shipment.

tags.description:
A list of labels or keywords associated with the shipment. Used to include additional
information to the shipment in order to differentiate, identify and categorise your shipments.

200.description: Shipment successfully created & tracking process has been started.

X-Shipsgo-Credits-Cost.description: The amount of credit consumption for the operation.

X-Shipsgo-Credits-Remaining.description: The amount of credit remaining after the operation.

402.description: Company does not have enought credits to create a shipment.

X-Shipsgo-Credits-Cost.description: The amount of credit consumption for the operation.

X-Shipsgo-Credits-Remaining.description: The amount of credit remaining after the operation.

get.description:
The shipments list endpoint allows you to retrieve a list of your shipments (**basic information**) including options
for applying various filters and sorting. To access detailed information (such as `route details`, `container movements`,
`followers`, etc.) about a shipment, use the **OCEAN - Details of the Shipment** endpoint.

**Example #1:** Ongoing (SAILING) shipments carried by **MSC** (MSCU) or **OOCL** (OOLU) with upcoming
arrivals, listed in order.

```plain
/ocean/shipments
  ?filters[status]=eq:SAILING
  &filters[carrier]=in:MSCU,OOLU
  &order_by=date_of_discharge,asc
```

**Example #2:** Shipments loaded from **India** (IN), between **September 1, 2024**, and **October 1, 2024**, listed in
order of loading date.

```plain
/ocean/shipments
  ?filters[port_of_loading_country]=eq:IN
  &filters[date_of_loading]=between:2024-09-01...2024-10-01
  &order_by=date_of_loading,asc
```

**Example #3:** Shipments tagged with `COMPANY_ABC` and created by **John Doe** (`john-doe@example.com`).

```plain
/ocean/shipments
  ?filters[tags]=with:COMPANY_ABC
  &filters[creator]=eq:john-doe@example.com
```

schema.description:
You can use the following filters with their respective operators. Common structure is `filters[field]=operator:value(s)`.

| Field                       | Operators                                 | Description                                                                                    |
| --------------------------- | ----------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `id`                        | `eq`                                      | Unique identifier for the shipment.                                                            |
| `status`                    | `eq`, `not_eq`, `in`, `not_in`            | Enum: `NEW`, `INPROGRESS`, `BOOKED`, `LOADED`, `SAILING`, `ARRIVED`,`DISCHARGED`, `UNTRACKED`. |
| `carrier`                   | `eq`, `not_eq`, `in`, `not_in`            | The SCAC code of the carrier(s). Example: `OOLU`, `MSCU`, `MAEU`, `HLCU`.                      |
| `container_number`          | `eq`                                      | Must be in format `AAAU7777777`.                                                               |
| `booking_number`            | `eq`                                      | Booking number of the shipment.                                                                |
| `reference`                 | `eq`, `contains`, `not_contains`          | Custom reference used to classify shipment(s).                                                 |
| `port_of_loading`           | `eq`, `not_eq`, `in`, `not_in`            | The code of the port(s). Example: `TRIST`, `USNYC`, `CNNIN`.                                   |
| `date_of_loading`           | `eq`, `gt`, `gte`, `lt`, `lte`, `between` | Loading date in ISO 8601 format.                                                               |
| `port_of_loading_country`   | `eq`, `not_eq`, `in`, `not_in`            | The unique 2-letter country code. Example: `TR`, `US`, `ES`.                                   |
| `port_of_discharge`         | `eq`, `not_eq`, `in`, `not_in`            | The code of the port(s). Example: `TRIST`, `USNYC`, `CNNIN`.                                   |
| `date_of_discharge`         | `eq`, `gt`, `gte`, `lt`, `lte`, `between` | Discharge date in ISO 8601 format.                                                             |
| `port_of_discharge_country` | `eq`, `not_eq`, `in`, `not_in`            | The unique 2-letter country code. Example: `TR`, `US`, `ES`.                                   |
| `followers`                 | `with`, `without`                         | Valid email addresses.                                                                         |
| `tags`                      | `with`, `without`                         | Labels or keywords associated with the shipment(s).                                            |
| `creator`                   | `eq`, `not_eq`, `in`, `not_in`            | Valid email addresses.                                                                         |
| `created_at`                | `eq`, `gt`, `gte`, `lt`, `lte`, `between` | Creation date in ISO 8601 format.                                                              |
| `updated_at`                | `eq`, `gt`, `gte`, `lt`, `lte`, `between` | Update date in ISO 8601 format.                                                                |
| `checked_at`                | `eq`, `gt`, `gte`, `lt`, `lte`, `between` | Last check date in ISO 8601 format.                                                            |
| `discarded_at`              | `eq`, `gt`, `gte`, `lt`, `lte`, `between` | Discard date in ISO 8601 format.                                                               |

You can sort the list with following fields. Common structure is `order_by=field,direction`.

| Field             | Description                                  |
| ----------------- | -------------------------------------------- |
| id                | Unique identifier for the shipment.          |
| status            | Current status of the shipment.              |
| date_of_loading   | Loading date of the shipment.                |
| date_of_discharge | Discharge date of the shipment.              |
| created_at        | Date and time the shipment was created.      |
| updated_at        | Date and time the shipment was last updated. |
| checked_at        | Date and time the shipment was last checked. |

**Note:** By default the results are ordered by the `created_at` in `desc` (descending) order.
The number of records to skip before returning results. Default: 0.
The number of records to return. Default: 25.

meta.description: Meta information about the returned list.

more.description: Indicates whether there are more resources to fetch.

total.description: Indicates the total number of resources, it's only available when there is no more resources.

This endpoint provides a GeoJSON FeatureCollection with all map-related data for a shipment, including port locations,
the vessel’s current position, and past/future paths.

GeoJSON ([RFC 7946](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7946)) is a format used to describe geographic data and can
be directly used with most mapping libraries (Leaflet, Mapbox, Google Maps, etc.).

In this endpoint, only `Point` and `LineString` geometry types are used. Each geometry is returned within a Feature as
defined below:

-   Each Feature has a `geometry` (`Point` or `LineString`).
-   Each Feature has a set of `properties` according to its `geometry`.
-   Each Feature has a `status` property, which can be:
    -   `PAST` – The shipment was at this port/route in the past.
    -   `CURRENT` – The shipment is at this port/route now.
    -   `FUTURE` – The shipment is expected to be at this port/route in the future.

#### Point Features

Features with `Point` geometry represent ports. Each `Point` Feature includes the port’s name, unlocode, timezone, and
country information.

#### LineString Features

Features with `LineString` geometry represent routes between two locations. Each `LineString` Feature includes vessel
information, voyage number, related events (`DEPA`, `ARRV`), and current position (when `status` is `CURRENT`).

tag.description: The new tag (a label or keyword) that was successfully added to the shipment.

403.description: User does not permitted for this action.

delete.summary: OCEAN - Remove Tag from the Shipment
delete.description: This endpoint allows users to remove an existing tag from an existing ocean shipment.

parameters.name: shipment_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the shipment.

parameters.name: tag_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the tag.

200.description: Tag successfully removed from the shipment.

403.description: User does not permitted for this action.

post.description: This event is triggered when a new shipment is created.

post.description:

`status`, `movements`, `tags`, `followers`, etc.
